concept: A young driver brings their car in for what seems like a routine check, but the mechanic's deadpan reactions to the absurd state of neglect turn a tense moment into a comedy of automotive denial.

shots.frame: CU: Jesse's hands gripping the steering wheel, knuckles white, dashboard warning lights glowing red in the background blur
shots.audio: Engine rattling, warning chime beeping insistently

shots.frame: MCU: Jesse's face through windshield as they pull into the auto shop, forced smile, eyes darting
shots.audio: Engine sputters to silence, distant pneumatic tools

shots.frame: CU: Mechanic's weathered hands opening the hood, steam rising, a long pause, then pulling out a sticky note covering the warning lights
shots.audio: Hood latch click, hiss of pressure, then: 'Is this... is this a Post-it?'

shots.frame: ECU: Mechanic's face, eyebrows raised to maximum height, looking at Jesse with theatrical disbelief
CU: Jesse's face, looking down, then slowly back up, a small nod of acknowledgment
MCU: Mechanic gesturing wildly at the engine, Jesse trying not to laugh, both shaking their heads
shots.audio: 'Next time maybe try... I don't know... NOT covering the problem with office supplies?'

invisibleWide: This isn't just about a car. Jesse's been ignoring warning signs in their life for months—relationships, health, responsibilities. The mechanic represents the moment when avoidance meets consequence, but also the possibility of repair.

stormCloud.detail: The sticky note covering the warning lights has a smiley face drawn on it.

platform.hook: Opening shot of white knuckles and red warning lights immediately signals tension
platform.loop: Yes — the circular nature of ignoring problems creates natural replay curiosity
platform.soundOff: Strong — visual storytelling through hands, eyes, and warning lights carries the narrative
